problem.title: Studying for the Italian driving license is hard.
problem.subtitle: Especially if Italian isn't your first language.
problem.card1_text: The official quiz is only in Italian. Complex technical terms make studying frustrating.
problem.card2_text: Between work and family, finding hours to study is tough. You need short but effective sessions.
problem.card3_text: Without a personalized method, remembering everything is nearly impossible.

giulia.title: Meet Giulia.
giulia.subtitle: Your personal AI instructor, always available.
giulia.description: Marco, Ahmed, Karim, Aisha... give your AI instructor any name you want. She answers in 8 languages, 24 hours a day, with clear explanations and citations from the Italian Highway Code.
giulia.feat1_text: Italian, Arabic, Urdu, Hindi, Chinese, Spanish, French, English.
giulia.feat2_text: From Giulia to Marco, from Ahmed to Aisha. Your choice.
giulia.feat3_text: Every answer cites articles from the Italian Highway Code.
giulia.chat_msg1: Hi Mohamed! I'm Giulia, your instructor. Ask me anything about the B license — from road signs to the rules of the road!
giulia.chat_user_msg: What's the difference between fermata, sosta and arresto? They confuse me.
giulia.chat_msg2_intro: Great question — these three confuse almost everyone! 👇
giulia.chat_msg2_end: is an involuntary stop (e.g. a red light or traffic). A fermata is a short, voluntary stop — you stay with the vehicle. A sosta is when you leave the vehicle parked and can walk away.
giulia.chat_msg2_tip: Memory trick: arresto = forced, fermata = brief, sosta = parked.

footer.disclaimer_text: Pursuant to Article 123 of the Italian Highway Code, PatenteYes is a digital study tool and does not replace mandatory training at authorized driving schools. Theory and practical training to obtain a driving license must be carried out at authorized and approved driving schools in Italy.
